_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved.

activity_name: Shoring installation and battered cuts (pool shell)

hazards.title: Excavation collapse during shoring install
hazards.risk: Worker in trench during shoring install crushed by sloughing wall before shoring is in place

controls.title: Where possible, install shoring panels from above — no worker in unshored trench
Shoring installer competent / trained; sequence per shoring system manufacturer
Excavator with lifting attachment for all shoring panels
No hand or limb under suspended panel

hazards.title: Battered cut failure
hazards.risk: Batter cut at insufficient angle for soil type; face slips onto worker below

controls.title: Batter angle confirmed against soil type table (1:1 stable clay, 1:1.5 sand/fill, engineer-confirmed where uncertain)
Daily batter face inspection; stop and reassess after rain or any sloughing

tasks: Pre-start: confirm soil type from excavation; confirm shoring system selection or batter angle against soil-type chart.
Toolbox: walk through install sequence with crew + excavator operator.
Excavator lifts panels into position; installer guides from above using ropes/spreader bar.
Install struts / waler per manufacturer; verify torque/lock per spec.
Daily inspection of shoring system + soil conditions before crew enters trench.
For battered cuts: verify angle matches plan; trim back if any sloughing observed.
Stop work and reassess after rain, vibration, or any movement.
Removal: reverse sequence; remove from above; backfill progressively as panels are pulled.
